# airnow

airnow is an [R](https://www.r-project.org/) package for querying and
retrieving air quality information from
[AirNow](https://www.airnow.gov/) via the [AirNow
API](https://docs.airnowapi.org/). Current and historical readings as
well as forecasts can be retrieved as tidy data frames.

## Installation

You can install the stable version of airnow from CRAN:

``` r
install.packages("airnow")
```

If you‚Äôd like to try out the development version of airnow, you can
install directly from GitHub:

``` r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("briandconnelly/airnow")
```

## Creating an API Token

The [AirNow API](https://docs.airnowapi.org/) is generally free to use.
The
[`set_airnow_token()`](https://briandconnelly.github.io/airnow/reference/get_airnow_token.md)
function can be used to help you create and configure your API token.

``` r
library(airnow)

set_airnow_token()
```

## Examples

### Current air quality in Seattle

The AirNow API allows you to query air conditions either by ZIP code or
latitude/longitude. Here, we‚Äôll get the current conditions in Seattle by
ZIP code:

``` r
library(airnow)

get_airnow_conditions(zip = "98101")
#> # A tibble: 2 √ó 11
#>   date_observed hour_obs‚Ä¶¬Ļ local‚Ä¶¬≤ repor‚Ä¶¬≥ state‚Ä¶‚Āī latit‚Ä¶‚ĀĶ longi‚Ä¶‚Ā∂ param‚Ä¶‚Ā∑   aqi
#>   <date>             <int> <fct>   <fct>   <fct>     <dbl>   <dbl> <fct>   <int>
#> 1 2022-10-31             6 PST     Seattl‚Ä¶ WA         47.6   -122. O3         27
#> 2 2022-10-31             6 PST     Seattl‚Ä¶ WA         47.6   -122. PM2.5      30
#> # ‚Ä¶ with 2 more variables: category_number <int>, category_name <fct>, and
#> #   abbreviated variable names ¬Ļ‚Äčhour_observed, ¬≤‚Äčlocal_time_zone,
#> #   ¬≥‚Äčreporting_area, ‚Āī‚Äčstate_code, ‚ĀĶ‚Äčlatitude, ‚Ā∂‚Äčlongitude, ‚Ā∑‚Äčparameter
```

### Find the site with the lowest air quality near Washington state

``` r
library(airnow)
library(dplyr)

get_airnow_area(
  box = c(-125.394211, 45.295897, -116.736984, 49.172497),
  verbose = TRUE
) |>
  slice_max(order_by = aqi, n = 1) |>
  select(site_name, site_agency, latitude, longitude, aqi, datetime_observed)
#> # A tibble: 1 √ó 6
#>   site_name         site_agency        latit‚Ä¶¬Ļ longi‚Ä¶¬≤   aqi datetime_observed  
#>   <fct>             <fct>                <dbl>   <dbl> <int> <dttm>             
#> 1 Clarkston-13th St Washington Depart‚Ä¶    46.4   -117.    56 2022-10-31 13:00:00
#> # ‚Ä¶ with abbreviated variable names ¬Ļ‚Äčlatitude, ¬≤‚Äčlongitude
```

## Disclaimer

This package and its author are not affiliated with
[AirNow](https://www.airnow.gov/) or its
[partners](https://www.airnow.gov/partners/). See the [Data Exchange
Guidelines](https://docs.airnowapi.org/docs/DataUseGuidelines.pdf) for
more details about this data set and how it should be used. Data are
typically refreshed once per hour. Please be kind to this service and
limit your request rate.

# Package index

## Managing AirNow API tokens

An API token allows you to interact with the AirNow API. Tokens can be
created at [airnowapi.org](https://docs.airnowapi.org).

- [`get_airnow_token()`](https://briandconnelly.github.io/airnow/reference/get_airnow_token.md)
  [`set_airnow_token()`](https://briandconnelly.github.io/airnow/reference/get_airnow_token.md)
  : Manage AirNow API Tokens

## Retrieving air quality data

Retrieve forecasts and current/historical air quality data

- [`get_airnow_conditions()`](https://briandconnelly.github.io/airnow/reference/get_airnow_conditions.md)
  : Get current air quality conditions
- [`get_airnow_area()`](https://briandconnelly.github.io/airnow/reference/get_airnow_area.md)
  : Get air quality data for a given region
- [`get_airnow_forecast()`](https://briandconnelly.github.io/airnow/reference/get_airnow_forecast.md)
  : Get air quality forecast

## AQI values

Convert AQI values to color or text representations

- [`aqi_color()`](https://briandconnelly.github.io/airnow/reference/aqi.md)
  [`aqi_descriptor()`](https://briandconnelly.github.io/airnow/reference/aqi.md)
  : Label AQI Values
